notes: Per-photo filename strategy: one LrExportSession per collection (single preset per run per Locked Decision #9). Each rendition renders with Lightroom's default name to the per-(collection,preset) destination directory, then LrFileUtils.move renames it to the slug-based filename during the rendition loop. Chose this over LR_tokenCustomString / token-based renaming because the renaming-tokens system does not support a per-photo custom string inside a single ExportSession, and a one-session-per-photo loop would be heavyweight. For overwrite collisions, the code pre-deletes any existing file at the destination before LrFileUtils.move (LR's move does not overwrite). Content Credentials apply() tries LR_embedContentCredentials first then LR_contentCredentials; both pcall-wrapped; no user-facing surface on failure. LR_renamingTokensOn=false so LR uses source filenames, which the move step overrides anyway. Filename fallback sequence is zero-padded %05d counter shared across the whole run.
assumptions: catalog:getActiveSources() returns a list-like table iterable via ipairs; filter by src:type() matching LrCollection/LrCollectionSet/LrPublishedCollection/LrPublishedCollectionSet.
LrDialogs.confirm(message, info, actionVerb, cancelVerb, otherVerb) returns 'ok' | 'cancel' | 'other'; used for both collision prompt and summary-with-Reveal.
LrFileUtils.move returns ok, err and does NOT overwrite an existing destination; delete-then-move handles overwrite case.
LrProgressScope:setCancelable(true) + :isCanceled() is how user cancellation surfaces mid-rendition loop.
rendition:waitForRender() returns ok, pathOrErr where path is the rendered file's absolute location.
rendition.photo field maps back to the LrPhoto object passed via photosToExport, usable as a Lua table key.
LrPathUtils.expandPath resolves ~ to the user home on macOS.
logger:enable('logfile') writes to ~/Documents/LrClassicLogs/ — standard LrLogger behavior.
